articles.excerpt: The Punisher actor and Spider-Man: Brand New Day star reflects on shooting his first Marvel movie after a decade on streaming
articles.body: Jon Bernthal has been playing the definitive version of Marvel's ruthless vigilante, Frank Castle, ever since he showed up in season 2 of Daredevil on Netflix back in 2016. From the very start, Bernthal brought a perfect blend of unpredictable, righteous violence to the anti-hero known as The Punisher , but on July 31, he faces his biggest challenge yet: a Marvel movie .
articles.author: Jake Kleinman

articles.title: Divergence escalates the wrong population: unanimous misses auto-pass
articles.excerpt: Divergence escalates the wrong population: unanimous misses auto-pass Agent Determinism Illusions (Part 7) Where this fits: This part does not continue Part 13's probe-vs-prose thread. It returns to P…
articles.body: Divergence escalates the wrong population: unanimous misses auto-pass Agent Determinism Illusions (Part 7) Where this fits: This part does not continue Part 13's probe-vs-prose thread. It returns to Part 6 's L2→L3 escalation rule — Dipankar's move of treating vote disagreement as the human-review signal. Alexey Spinov's follow-up comment says that signal points at the wrong population. Two experiments check whether he is right, and what to put in the tripwire instead. Part 6 drew this control flow: L2 multi-perspective votes │ unanimous ──────────► AUTO-PASS / AUTO-REJECT │ divergence (e.g. 2–1) ► L3 human The caveat was already in the text: divergence measures ambiguity; it does not fix unanimous systematic bias.
articles.author: zxpmail

articles.title: Stop Running `terraform apply` From Your Laptop: Building Your First Terraform CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions
articles.excerpt: One of the biggest mistakes beginners make when learning Terraform is treating their local machine as the deployment server. A typical workflow looks like this: terraform init terraform plan terraform…
articles.body: One of the biggest mistakes beginners make when learning Terraform is treating their local machine as the deployment server. A typical workflow looks like this: terraform init terraform plan terraform apply While this approach is perfectly fine for learning, it quickly becomes problematic when working on real-world projects with multiple engineers. Consider these questions: Who deployed the infrastructure? Was the infrastructure reviewed before deployment? Can someone else reproduce the deployment? What happens if the engineer's laptop is lost or misconfigured? How do we know exactly what changed? These are some of the reasons Infrastructure as Code (IaC) is almost always integrated with Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines in professional environments.
articles.author: Ibrahim Oyinkolade

articles.title: I Built a Self-Hostable URL Shortener Because Bitly Now Charges $10/Month for Basic Links
articles.excerpt: In 2023, Bitly's free plan gave you 100 links per month with full analytics. In 2025, they slashed it to 5 links per month. Five. And they started showing full-screen interstitial ads before redirecti…
articles.body: In 2023, Bitly's free plan gave you 100 links per month with full analytics. In 2025, they slashed it to 5 links per month . Five. And they started showing full-screen interstitial ads before redirecting — even on links you created years ago. In 2026, their cheapest paid plan is $10/month — just to shorten links without ads. I was paying $120/year to turn long URLs into short ones. A problem that takes 3 lines of code to solve. So I solved it myself. For free. Forever. Introducing ZipLink ZipLink is a fast, self-hostable URL shortener built with Next.js and Firebase. Deploy it once, use it forever. No monthly fees. No link limits. No ads. No "upgrade to unlock analytics." Your links. Your data. Your domain. Your rules.
articles.author: Shivansh Goel

articles.title: I Built a Git-Based Decision Tracker — Branch Your Life Choices, Commit Predictions, and Let Time Score Your Judgment
articles.excerpt: Every developer knows the power of git branch. You experiment without fear, revert mistakes, and merge only what works. But what if you could do the same thing with life decisions? That's exactly what…
articles.body: Every developer knows the power of git branch . You experiment without fear, revert mistakes, and merge only what works. But what if you could do the same thing with life decisions ? That's exactly what I built. And it's called Forkcast . The Problem Nobody Talks About We all face major crossroads: Should I take this job offer or stay? Should I learn Rust or Go next? Should I co-found this startup or keep freelancing? Should I move cities for that opportunity? We make these calls based on gut feeling, maybe a pros/cons list, and then... we never look back to evaluate how good our judgment actually was. Think about it. Do you actually know if you're a good decision-maker? Or do you just assume you are because you can't remember the bad calls? There's no version control for decisions.
articles.author: Shivansh Goel

articles.title: Building MaxOS #6: The Architecture Is Finished. Now It's Time to Build.
articles.excerpt: A few weeks ago, MaxOS was just an idea sitting in my notes. Today, I finished designing the architecture that will power it. Ironically, I still haven't written much application code. And I think tha…
articles.body: A few weeks ago, MaxOS was just an idea sitting in my notes. Today, I finished designing the architecture that will power it. Ironically, I still haven't written much application code. And I think that was the right decision. Looking Back When I started this project, I thought I'd spend most of my time inside Cursor writing TypeScript. Instead, I've spent days designing. At first, that felt like I wasn't making progress. Now I realize this was the progress. Because MaxOS isn't just another Electron application. It's something I hope will grow for years. What Has Been Designed Over the last week I've completed the first version of the project's architecture.
articles.author: Max Paardekam

articles.title: Post-Quantum DNS and TLS: What ML-DSA Means for Your Site
articles.excerpt: Short version: you don't need to do anything urgent today, and your small site is not about to break because of quantum computers. The switch to post-quantum cryptography is happening at the layer you…
articles.body: Short version: you don't need to do anything urgent today, and your small site is not about to break because of quantum computers. The switch to post-quantum cryptography is happening at the layer your host and browser handle for you — your job is to pick providers who are on it, and to know how to read the new DNSSEC error codes when something goes wrong. Cloudflare recently made two moves worth paying attention to. They argued for adopting ML-DSA (a post-quantum signature standard) sooner rather than later, and after a top-level domain outage they shipped new DNSSEC error codes so operators can actually see what failed. Both are plumbing changes — the quiet kind that can take a site offline without warning. So it's worth understanding what's yours to worry about and what isn't.
articles.author: TPC Hosting

articles.title: Why Most RAG Systems Fail in Production: The Hidden Architecture Problems Behind AI Search
articles.excerpt: Building a reliable RAG system is not about connecting an LLM to a vector database. It requires a complete architecture covering data ingestion, retrieval, ranking, evaluation, and production operatio…
articles.body: Building a reliable RAG system is not about connecting an LLM to a vector database. It requires a complete architecture covering data ingestion, retrieval, ranking, evaluation, and production operations. This article is the first part of a five-part series where we will explore how modern RAG systems are designed, why naive implementations fail, and what it takes to build AI search systems that work reliably in production.
articles.author: Damir Karimov

articles.title: Avatar: Legends needs a fighting game crossover with James Cameron’s Avatar
articles.excerpt: Avatar: Legends already plays like Marvel vs. Capcom, so its only logical sequel would pit Aang and Korra against Jake Sully and the Na’vi.
articles.body: Never speak to me about anything called “Avatar,” unless you’re going to immediately clarify whether we are talking about blue people or a bald boy . Having worked in the entertainment journalism industry for more than 15 years, I’ve developed an eye twitch over this frequent point of confusion. Referring to James Cameron ’s franchise as “Papyrus Avatar” is also acceptable.
articles.author: Corey Plante

articles.title: Should Artemis IV astronauts actually land near the moon's south pole? Artemis II pilot says the lunar equator might be better
articles.excerpt: NASA has long eyed the moon's south pole for its first crewed Artemis landing. But Artemis II astronaut Victor Glover says the agency should target an easier and safer place to touch down.
articles.body: The moon's south pole has long been the target of NASA's Artemis lunar landing missions, but one Artemis astronaut is questioning if that's still the best plan. NASA's Victor Glover, who piloted the Artemis II mission around the moon this past April, proposed a different approach while speaking at the NASA Exploration Science Forum at the agency's Ames Research Center in California on July 21, according to a report published on Space News . "I think we need to be realistic and work our way to the south pole, and maybe not try to go there first," Glover said.

A new “Notifications” option will also be added to Chrome’s site controls UI. Google has been hard at work improving the user experience of Chrome on Android. The company recently added new features, including a redesigned navigation bar . Now, it’s working on a change that will make pretty much every internet user breathe a sigh of relief. In a post on the Chrome for Developers blog, the company announced that it’s making some changes to how the browser displays notification prompts on Android (via PiunikaWeb ).

articles.title: A cheaper Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 could help solve phone price hikes (maybe)
articles.excerpt: With next-gen chip prices very likely to soar, a new SM8850-1-AB variant could offer near-flagship performance for cheaper.
articles.body: Credit: Qualcomm TL;DR Qualcomm is rumored to be preparing a new Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 model (SM8850-1-AB) to live alongside three existing variants. The new chip will offer near-standard benchmark performance at a lower cost, targeting phones under 4,000 CNY (~$600). Phone makers may turn to this refreshed SoC amid rising RAM and component costs that are driving up the price of next-gen chips and phones. We’re almost done with major flagship launches for this generation, which means we’re also inching closer to the launch of the next generation of flagship processors. Qualcomm is expected to launch the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 and 8 Elite Gen 6 Pro at the Snapdragon Summit in September, but the company could have one more surprise in tow.
articles.author: Aamir Siddiqui

Instead of stealing a real identity, the attacker manufactures a new one, frankensteining together several real data points with fabricated ones to create a person who doesn't exist. articles.excerpt: Nintendo legend Shigeru Miyamoto has defended the company's remakes, and said that newer versions of classic titles can lead to "future developments" — something that fans are keen to see for Star Fox…
articles.body: Nintendo legend Shigeru Miyamoto has defended the company's remakes, and said that newer versions of classic titles can lead to "future developments" — something that fans are keen to see for Star Fox and Zelda. While Nintendo remaking and re-releasing its classic games is absolutely nothing new, it's fair to point out that two of the company's biggest Switch 2 games this year will be very familiar to long-term fans. Star Fox , which launched last month, sparked conversation online for technically being the company's third reimaging of its N64 classic . The upcoming Switch 2 incarnation of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time , meanwhile, will follow various re-releases of the N64 original, as well as its 3DS port.

articles.title: Pokémon TCG: Ascended Heroes Sealed Booster Bundles Back in Stock at Amazon
articles.excerpt: Pokémon TCG Ascended Heroes Booster Bundles are back in stock at Amazon. But is it worth the price, and can you find a better deal elsewhere?
articles.body: While we're all still reeling over the mismanagement of the recent Pokémon 30th Anniversary Celebration set preorders and 151 shambolic drops at The Pokémon Center, let us perhaps return some attention to the recent restocks that are happening at Amazon right now. Everything is still listed above MSRP, but the retail giant has quickly become one of the most consistent places to find stock that isn't from a dodgy third-party seller , and will actually arrive in decent condition, fast. We've recently seen Pitch Black ETBs and boosters restocked at around market price, and now we're seeing something similar with one of the year's most popular sets so far, Ascended Heroes. Ascended Heroes Booster Bundles are currently back in stock at Amazon, listed for $95.88.

articles.title: The James Webb Space Telescope's disappearing 'Little Red Dots' may lead to another cosmic puzzle
articles.excerpt: Just as the dinosaurs didn't disappear but rather evolved into birds, cosmic dinosaurs in the form of the JWST's Little Red Dots may fade away completely.
articles.body: Paleontologists now know that many of the dinosaurs didn't disappear but instead evolved into modern birds, and new research suggests that "cosmic dinosaurs" observed by the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) didn't go extinct either. Rather, they may have evolved into familiar sights in the modern universe: vast conglomerations of densely packed stars called "globular clusters." Little Red Dots became quite the puzzle for astronomers in 2022, when the JWST began to routinely spot them in abundance around 600 million years after the Big Bang . That is because these objects seemingly disappear before the cosmos gets to around 2 billion years old.
articles.author: Robert Lea

The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing recovery methods to log in to an account, including an email address or a phone number. The idea is to use a video selfie as a way to regain access if a user ever gets locked out or doesn't have access

articles.title: Elon Musk hints at Tesla (TSLA)-SpaceX merger on earnings call
articles.excerpt: Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls. He stopped short of confirming anythi…
articles.body: Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls. He stopped short of confirming anything, deferring to Tesla’s general counsel and “the appropriate process” — but the exchange is the closest Musk has come to addressing the merger speculation directly.

articles.title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
articles.excerpt: Orienspace's Gravity-1, the world's most powerful solid rocket, aced its third-ever mission on Tuesday (July 21), launching nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea.
articles.body: The world's most powerful solid-fuel rocket just aced its third-ever mission. Gravity-1, a distinctively squat and stubby rocket , launched Tuesday night (July 21) from the deck of a ship stationed off the coast of Shanghai. Liftoff occurred at 10:54 p.m. EDT (0254 GMT and 10:54 a.m. Beijing time on July 22), according to the state-run Chinese outlet Xinhua, which further reported that all nine satellites reached "their designated orbits." Details about those satellites were not provided. Orienspace's Gravity-1 rocket launches nine satellites to orbit from a ship at sea on July 21, 2026. (Image credit: CCTV) Gravity-1 is operated by Orienspace, a company based in the eastern Chinese province of Shandong.
